item.title: Prefiled for introduction.
item.description: Prefiled for introduction.

item.title: New laws proposed for WA's 2026 legislative session | FOX 13 Seattle
item.description: News article "New laws proposed for WA's 2026 legislative session | FOX 13 Seattle" from FOX13.

item.title: First reading, referred to Law & Justice.
item.description: First reading, referred to Law & Justice.

item.title: A different sort of speech entirely
item.description: News article "A different sort of speech entirely" from The Washington Observer.

item.title: 👍Mark McKechnie - Washington Traffic Safety Commission
item.description: Mark McKechnie signed up to testify Pro. They are signed up to represent Washington Traffic Safety Commission.

item.title: ❓James McMahan - WA Assoc Sheriffs & Police Chiefs
item.description: James McMahan signed up to testify Other. They are signed up to represent WA Assoc Sheriffs & Police Chiefs.

item.title: LAW - Majority; 1st substitute bill be substituted, do pass.
item.description: LAW - Majority; 1st substitute bill be substituted, do pass.

item.title: Minority; without recommendation.
item.description: Minority; without recommendation.

item.title: Passed to Rules Committee for second reading.
item.description: Passed to Rules Committee for second reading.

item.title: Placed on second reading by Rules Committee.
item.description: Placed on second reading by Rules Committee.

item.title: 1st substitute bill not substituted.
item.description: 1st substitute bill not substituted.

item.title: Floor amendment(s) adopted.
item.description: Floor amendment(s) adopted.

item.title: Rules suspended. Placed on Third Reading.
item.description: Rules suspended. Placed on Third Reading.

item.title: Third reading, passed; yeas, 45; nays, 3; absent, 0; excused, 1.
item.description: Third reading, passed; yeas, 45; nays, 3; absent, 0; excused, 1.

item.title: First reading, referred to Community Safety.
item.description: First reading, referred to Community Safety.

item.title: 👍James McMahan - WA Assoc Sheriffs & Police Chiefs
item.description: James McMahan signed up to testify Pro. They are signed up to represent WA Assoc Sheriffs & Police Chiefs.

item.title: 👍Mark McKechnie - Washington Traffic Safety Commission
item.description: Mark McKechnie signed up to testify Pro. They are signed up to represent Washington Traffic Safety Commission.

item.title: 👎JJ Hodgins - Washington Association of Criminal Defense Lawyers & Washington Defender Association
item.description: JJ Hodgins signed up to testify Con. They are signed up to represent Washington Association of Criminal Defense Lawyers & Washington Defender Association.

item.title: 👍💵Russell Brown - WA Association of Prosecuting Attorneys
item.description: Russell Brown signed up to testify Pro. They are signed up to represent WA Association of Prosecuting Attorneys. They are a registered lobbyist.

item.title: 👍💵Chad Campbell - IUOE Local 612
item.description: Chad Campbell signed up to testify Pro. They are signed up to represent IUOE Local 612. They are a registered lobbyist.

item.title: 👍Thomas DuBeau - Operating Engineers, IUOE Local 302
item.description: Thomas DuBeau signed up to testify Pro. They are signed up to represent Operating Engineers, IUOE Local 302.

item.title: 👍Jerry VanderWood - Associated General Contractors (AGC)
item.description: Jerry VanderWood signed up to testify Pro. They are signed up to represent Associated General Contractors (AGC).

item.title: CS - Executive action taken by committee.
item.description: CS - Executive action taken by committee.

item.title: CS - Majority; do pass with amendment(s).
item.description: CS - Majority; do pass with amendment(s).

item.title: Referred to Appropriations.
item.description: Referred to Appropriations.

item.title: Washington state eyes tougher penalties for 30+ mph speeding - Axios Seattle
item.description: News article "Washington state eyes tougher penalties for 30+ mph speeding - Axios Seattle" from Axios.

item.title: By resolution, returned to Senate Rules Committee for third reading.
item.description: By resolution, returned to Senate Rules Committee for third reading.
item.guid: 2026-03-12/By resolution, returned to Senate Rules Committee for third reading.
